search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk ng115 COPD exacerbation features increased breathlessness cough sputum
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic exacerbation diagnosis or severity class
oxygen, antibiotics, steroids, bronchodilator and admission recommendations
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The diagnosis and severity remain clinician-entered.
Treatment, medicine, dose and disposition are outside documentation-only scope.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with clinician-established COPD and a possible acute exacerbation; asthma, heart failure, pneumonia, pulmonary embolism and other mimics require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: NICE covers community and hospital COPD care; only symptom-change documentation is mapped to ED use.
UAE_applicability: Clinical descriptors transfer, but UAE emergency, respiratory, antimicrobial, oxygen, admission and follow-up policies apply.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG115 recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact NICE exacerbation-features section supports documenting onset or change, severity or functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician risk context in a person with clinician-established COPD; it does not diagnose an exacerbation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of worsening breathlessness, cough, sputum production, and sputum colour change as exacerbation features without asserting an exacerbation diagnosis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Follow-up and review recommendations were examined for baseline context but were not treated as exact emergency-exacerbation management evidence.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports at least annual review of smoking, breathlessness, exercise tolerance, exacerbations, pulmonary rehabilitation, complications, medicine effects, inhaler technique, referral need, FEV1 and FVC, BMI, MRC dyspnoea, oxygen saturation, cor pulmonale, nutrition, mood, and social needs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Baseline COPD severity, oxygen targets, blood gas, imaging, infection context, examination, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, dose, response, observation, admission and discharge remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
